# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

```ts:start_example
import * as jwt from "jwt-simple";
const express = require("express");
export const app = express();
app.use(decodeTokenFromJWTMiddleware); // middleware
async function decodeTokenFromJWTMiddleware(req, res, next) {
  try {
    req["user"] = await jwtDecode<object>(req?.headers?.authorization || "");
  } catch (error) {}
  return next();
}
export type JwtDecodedType = [string]; 
// export interface JwtDecodedType {
//   exp?: number; // expired timestamp in Unixtime,
//   iat?: number; // issued timestamp in Unixtime,
// } 
async function jwtDecode(jwtToken: string) {
  const token = await new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) =>
    jwt.decode(jwtToken as any, resolve)
  );
}
export default app;
```
